title: DQ Platform Rule Schema v2
description: Canonical JSON Schema for rule YAMLs at schema version 2. v2 extends v1 with mode-as-primitive (per ADR-0021), explicit source descriptor (per ADR-0023), per-source-type substrate parameters including the kafka window sub-object (per ADR-0024), and per-check kind discipline with optional params (per ADR-0022). The byte-identical mirror at rules/_schema/v2.schema.json is enforced by the schema-mirror CI gate. The mirror is never edited by hand; use `make sync-schema` to update it.

version.description: Schema version this rule was written against. Mandatory per ADR-0001 C4. Engine and linter reject rules without it.

entity.description: Entity identifier. ASCII alphanumeric plus underscore, dot, hyphen. The pipe character is explicitly forbidden per ADR-0002 input-safety (entity is one of the five inputs to the execution_id hash; the formula is pipe-separated with no escaping).

mode.description: Evaluation mode for the entity. set evaluates a bounded set of rows over a closed window per ADR-0021; record evaluates each incoming record independently. Per ADR-0021 §C-B0S1.3 mode is the primitive: source.type, runner, and the catalog kind prefix must all agree with the value here. The linter enforces the agreement; the engine loader rejects mode values it does not yet support.

source.description: Source descriptor for the entity. The type field discriminates the substrate; per-substrate fields are validated by the matching oneOf branch. Per ADR-0023 source.type must align with mode (bigquery → set, kafka → record); the linter enforces this cross-check.

checks.description: Non-empty array of check definitions. Each check is evaluated independently per ADR-0004 always-continue.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the rule.

sourceBigQuery.description: BigQuery source descriptor for set-mode entities. project_id, dataset_id, table_id are mandatory; partition_column is optional and signals partition-pruning intent to the engine query planner per P4 cost discipline.

partition_column.description: Optional partition column name. When set, the engine applies partition-pruning predicates to set-mode queries per P4 cost discipline.

sourceKafka.description: Kafka source descriptor for record-mode entities per ADR-0024. topic, consumer_group, and window are mandatory. window carries the watermark-bounded tumbling boundary that closes a record-mode execution.

consumer_group.description: Consumer group identifier per ADR-0024. The engine binds one consumer per (entity, consumer_group) and tracks per-partition offsets under it.

kafkaWindow.description: Tumbling watermark-bounded window per ADR-0024. type is the boundary kind; duration is the window length; lateness_tolerance is the watermark grace period before the window closes and emits an execution. Durations follow the duration grammar enforced by the linter.

type.description: Window boundary kind. v2 supports tumbling only; non-tumbling boundaries are deferred to a future schema version per ADR-0024.

duration.description: Window duration as a duration literal (e.g., 1m, 5m, 1h). The linter validates the grammar.

lateness_tolerance.description: Watermark grace period after window-end before the engine closes the window and emits an execution. Same duration grammar as duration.

check_id.description: Check identifier; component of the dq_check_results composite key per ADR-0003 CC7. ASCII alphanumeric plus underscore, dot, hyphen; pipe forbidden.

kind.description: Discriminator for the check's evaluation strategy. Per ADR-0022 v2 requires a mode-prefixed kind: `set.<name>` for set-mode checks, `record.<name>` for record-mode checks. The linter cross-checks the prefix against the rule's mode and against the catalog's per-kind source_mode entry.

params.description: Optional per-kind parameter block. The schema does not enumerate shapes here; the catalog (engine/internal/dsl/catalog/v1.yaml) declares each kind's params_schema, and the linter validates params against the matching catalog entry per ADR-0022.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the check.
